steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage notes that some indigenous language expressions appear only in songs, yet current preservation efforts focus exclusively on spoken language. The question asks what the researchers must conclude about what needs to happen next.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘acknowledge that Indigenous songs are largely but not entirely composed using phrases taken from spoken language.’: the passage says the opposite—some expressions appear only in songs, not in everyday speech. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘recognize that Indigenous languages likely have more unique expressions that represent ecological knowledge than represent other types of information.’: the passage doesn't limit the issue to ecological knowledge; the concern is about language expressions broadly. This narrows the claim without support. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘analyze musical similarities in the songs of the Warlpiri and Temiar people.’: analysis is not the same as preservation. The passage flags a preservation gap, not a research gap. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘incorporate the preservation of songs into their broader efforts to protect Indigenous languages.’: directly addresses the gap between where unique expressions live (songs) and where current efforts focus (spoken language). ✓
That confirms D is correct.
